title: Cross-session roadmap tools — complete blueprint + harden edge cases
boundary: Roadmap tooling (blueprint + 5 tool files + audit trail)
consumer_purpose: Sessions can read the full roadmap blueprint, track progress, query next actions, deprecate obsolete items, and reprioritize dynamically — all through audited tools.
claim_atoms: roadmap_init falls back to active.v1.json if missing blueprint
roadmap_progress preserves existing completion_pct when omitted
all 5 tools write to audit trail correctly
content:
## Step 1: Create blueprint file
Create `docs/json/roadmaps/opencode-desktop-phase3-roadmap.v1.json` from active.v1.json items.
- Strip session tracking arrays (reset to initial empty state)
- Keep all phases, items, depends_on, effort, context_summary, next_step
- Set all statuses to not_started (except completed ones stay completed for CI-001..003)
- Schema: { schema_version: "v1", title, phases, items }

## Step 2: roadmap_init — fallback to active.v1.json
- If blueprint file doesn't exist, try reading active.v1.json instead
- If neither exists, return empty roadmap with suggestion

## Step 3: roadmap_progress — optional completion_pct + note truncation
- completion_pct: change from `args.completion_pct ?? 0` to `args.completion_pct ?? item.completion_pct ?? 0`
- notes > 500 chars: truncate with "..." marker

## Step 4: roadmap_next — show_blocked parameter
- Add `show_blocked` optional boolean arg
- When true, include blocked items in results with their unmet_dependencies listed
- When false (default), existing behavior unchanged

## Step 5: roadmap_deprecate — re-deprecation + TTL extension
- If item already deprecated, extend TTL (30 days from now)
- Allow reason override when re-deprecating
- Keep existing replacement unless new one provided

## Step 6: roadmap_prioritize — auto-create phase
- If phase arg given and doesn't exist in active.phases, create it automatically with warning
- Include the phase name in changes output
